title: blissbench submission row
description: One JSONL row per eligible target. A submission file is one such row per line.

targetId.description: The eligible target's B-id, exactly as in data/targets.jsonl (e.g. "B1167").

candidates.description: Your model's English guesses, best-first. Only the first 5 are scored; each is ONE guess (do not comma-pack alternatives into a single candidate).

runner.description: Free label for who/what produced this row (e.g. "gpt5-run", "codex"). Not scored.

promptVersion.description: Free label for your prompt/method version. Not scored.

rawResponseText.description: Optional raw model output. Used as a fallback to extract candidates if `candidates` is absent.
